title: I built a free multiplayer game where AI trading agents compete against each other on real market data — and you can import your own
author: Adept_Cat_4977
selftext:
Hey everyone,

I've been building a side project called **VEGA** — a web-based multiplayer trading simulation where you create AI trading agents and compete against other players on real market data.

The idea is simple: pick a market, build your agent, launch it, and see how it ranks against everyone else.

**How it works:**

You choose one of 6 trading desks — Equities, Forex, Commodities, Rates & Credit, Quant, or Crypto — each with \~10 real instruments. Then you configure your agent's strategy: select technical indicators (RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, moving averages...), set your entry/exit thresholds, and define your risk management rules.

Once launched, your agent trades autonomously on real historical market data (via yfinance) and you get full performance metrics: P&L, win rate, Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, equity curve — the whole deal.

Then you compete. Free tournaments, leaderboards, and rankings.

**The part I'm most excited about: you can import your own AI agent.** If you've built your own trading algorithm in Python, you can bring it into VEGA and pit it against other players' agents. You're not limited to the built-in indicator configurator — the platform is designed to let custom agents compete on equal footing. Whether you've trained an ML model, written a rules-based system, or coded something completely experimental, you can test it against real humans and their strategies.

**What's live right now:**

* 6 desks with real market data
* Agent configuration through the UI (no coding required for beginners)
* Custom agent import for more advanced users
* Free tournaments and global leaderboard
* Full performance dashboard with TradingView charts

**What's coming next:**

* Buy-in tournaments (small stakes from $0.50 to $5, winner takes the pot)
* 1v1 duels — challenge any player directly
* Agent replay — step through every decision your agent made, with confidence scores and reasoning
* Scenario lab — test your agent on historical events (COVID crash, crypto winter, tech bull run...)
* Seasons with tiers and rankings (Bronze to Legend)
* Agent marketplace — publish your best agents for others to use (black box, code never exposed)
* Special events (Flash Crash Friday, Crypto Weekend Madness, Speed Trading Sprint...)

No real money required to play. It's a training ground — hence the name: trading meets training.

Tech stack if you're curious: Next.js, TypeScript, Python serverless trading engine, Prisma + Postgres, TradingView Lightweight Charts, NextAuth, deployed on Vercel.

Still early stage. I'm actively developing and would genuinely love feedback — on the concept, the UX, the agent system, anything.

[**https://vega-trading.vercel.app/**](https://vega-trading.vercel.app/)
